sources.name: Open Language Profiles English datasets from CEFR-J
sources.licenseNote: CEFR-J vocabulary profile is permitted for research and commercial use with citation per repository terms.
sources.transform: Uppercased, deduped, manually filtered for party play, then pooled and auto-classified into no-tag green/yellow/red decks.

sources.name: Curated party-game additions
sources.licenseNote: Original short list of common party-game words and phrases authored for this repo.
sources.transform: Uppercased, deduped, pooled with CEFR candidates, and auto-classified into no-tag green/yellow/red/money decks.

sources.name: ESDB / SCOWL American English wordlists
sources.licenseNote: SCOWLv2 permits use, copy, modification, distribution, and sale of word lists created from it when the copyright and permission notice are retained in supporting documentation.
sources.transform: Downloaded en_US and en_US-large 2026.02.25 from en-wl/wordlist-diff; kept lowercase alphabetic entries only; removed unsafe, duplicate, near-duplicate, too-short, and too-long entries; prioritized quality before audit-bucketing.

sources.name: CMU Pronouncing Dictionary
sources.licenseNote: Use for research or commercial purposes is unrestricted; redistribution requests acknowledgement of Carnegie Mellon University origin.
sources.transform: Used as the primary pronounceability filter and syllable signal so generated entries are practical to say aloud during play.

sources.name: Open English WordNet 2025
sources.licenseNote: Released under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC-BY 4.0).
sources.transform: Used as a part-of-speech and semantic-attestation signal; also fills the remaining single-word target count after CMUdict-filtered ESDB entries.

audit.ruleBasis: Sorted selected single-word candidates by a 25 Words or Less difficulty score: clueability under 5-word boards, tight clue-word budgets, stack point risk, pronunciation evidence, source commonness, length, syllables, abstract morphology, inflection noise, and part of speech. Money entries are also single words, selected from clean, common, pronounceable candidates and excluded from the color decks.
